headline: K-12 school funding recalibration bill becomes law without Gordon’s signature
summary: Wyoming Gov. Mark Gordon allowed a K-12 funding recalibration bill to become law without his signature, warning that its "instructional silo" restrictions disadvantage small districts by limiting operational funding for transportation, safety, and nutrition. He argued the approach usurps local school board authority and could force rural districts to cut non-instructional services. While supporters claim the restrictions will boost teacher salaries, Gordon maintained that operational and instructional needs are inseparable.

headline: Hundreds testify in favor of ICE accountability, ‘safe spaces’ for immigrants
summary: The Connecticut legislature is considering two bills limiting federal immigration enforcement: Senate Bill 91 establishes "protected areas" (schools, hospitals, churches) where civil arrests are prohibited and authorizes the state Inspector General to investigate use-of-force by federal agents; S.B. 397 allows state-level lawsuits against federal agents for constitutional violations and restricts qualified immunity. The measures address Trump administration reversals of prior enforcement restrictions and documented declines in school attendance and healthcare access due to immigrant fear of detention. Democratic lawmakers and advocacy groups support the bills for community safety and rights protection, while Republican legislators argue they may nullify federal immigration law. The state Inspector General endorsed the oversight provisions, noting his office is equipped to investigate federal agents and would have statutory authority to access evidence, though concerns about federal cooperation persist. If adopted, Connecticut would join several states with similar immigration enforcement restrictions.
